surface.name: Celonis Platform role-based getting-started routing
surface.selection_basis: Celonis publishes a general Getting started with the Celonis Platform page, but it explicitly says the starting product areas and features depend on the user's business role and license. It routes to five materially different task families and requires every user to belong to a team, so the official docs do not select one universal first-mile surface or success terminal.

alternatives_considered.name: Set up and manage a Celonis Platform team
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is an administrator route whose success is user access, not a universal developer or analyst outcome.

alternatives_considered.name: Integrate data sources
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires selecting a source system, extraction mechanism, permissions, and data model, and current release notes say the former platform Quickstarts were removed.

alternatives_considered.name: Create applications and analyze data
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This assumes appropriate Studio access and data rather than providing a universal no-account first path.

alternatives_considered.name: View dashboards and use applications
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a consumer route whose first success depends on content already made available by the developer's team.

alternatives_considered.name: Manage user profile
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Profile management is an account task, not the platform's first value-producing developer outcome.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start without assuming a Celonis team, role, license, data source, application, or existing content, identify the official general first-mile route, and follow it to the platform's first documented success.
assumed_prior_state: No Celonis account, team membership, invitation, open-signup link, SSO configuration, role, license seat, permissions, data source, data pool, process model, application, or dashboard is assumed.
A browser, working business identity, and network connection are available.
No account was created, invitation was accepted, SSO or 2FA was completed, team was joined, data was connected, content was created, or application was opened during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: No single platform-wide first success is established by the general Celonis getting-started documentation.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A human must choose whether first success means team administration, data integration, app or analysis creation, consuming an existing dashboard or app, or profile management before a chronological route can be researched.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: No universal completion signal is documented; each role and task family has a different result and may expose different product features based on the team's license.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The general page states that how a user gets started depends on their business role, lists five main task families, and warns that available features depend on the Celonis Platform license.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Choosing a route would determine the actor, required permissions, access method, inputs, and value terminal. Selecting one without a user objective would substitute researcher judgment for the official platform-wide documentation, especially because the release notes say the former data-integration Quickstarts were removed.

prerequisites.requirement: Belong to at least one Celonis Platform team; a team controls who can access it and what permissions users receive.
Use the team's configured sign-in method: default Celonis ID with email-delivered 2FA token, SAML SSO, or OIDC SSO.
Have the role and permissions required by the selected task family.
Have a Celonis Platform license that exposes the product features required by the selected task.

primary_path.action: Open Getting started with the Celonis Platform and review the role-based starting routes.
details: The page offers team administration, data integration and task mining, application creation and analysis, dashboard and application use, and profile management.
It says available product areas and features depend on business role and license.
primary_path.output: The five official task families and their access dependencies are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The documentation makes the required role and task choice explicit.

primary_path.action: Stop before selecting a task family or attempting team access because no universal role, license, team, or first-success outcome is established.
details: All users must belong to at least one team.
The team's access route can be administrator invitation, administrator-enabled open signup, default Celonis ID, SAML, or OIDC.
A current release note says the former data-integration Quickstarts were removed from the platform.
primary_path.input: The unresolved role, task, team, license, and access context
primary_path.output: No route is selected and no external state is changed.
primary_path.success_signal: The record preserves the official decision boundary instead of promoting one task to a universal first mile.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A human objective and account context are required to select a defensible chronological path.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The official general guide requires a choice among setting up and managing a team, integrating data sources and capturing interactions, creating applications and analyzing data, viewing dashboards and using applications, or managing a profile. Before any route, the user must belong to a team through an administrator invitation or an administrator-enabled open-signup link and then use Celonis ID with email 2FA, SAML, or OIDC as configured. The selected team's license and the user's role and permissions determine which features are visible. The data-integration family additionally requires a source-system route and cannot rely on the former platform Quickstarts because current 2026 release notes say those options were removed.
candidate_path_gap.impact: There is no single actor, access sequence, input set, or success terminal to encode. A human must first define the intended role and value outcome, and then provide or select the relevant team and licensed surface before an end-to-end first-mile path can be fact-checked.

branches.condition: The user is a team administrator
branches.path: Follow team setup, security, invitations, roles, permissions, licenses, and activity administration.
branches.effect_on_first_success: First success becomes an administrative access or governance outcome rather than a data or application outcome.

branches.path: Choose a source-system and extraction route, create or select data-pool and modeling resources, run extraction and transformation work, and monitor it under the available license and permissions.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Introduces source credentials, system-specific setup, extraction choices, data modeling, and a data-oriented terminal.

branches.condition: The user creates applications or analyzes data
branches.path: Use the licensed Studio and data assets available to the team to create the relevant package, app, View, or Analysis.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Assumes existing access and data, and changes the terminal to authored analytical content.

branches.path: Open dashboards or applications already shared with the team and use the supplied process content.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Depends on another user having already prepared and shared content.

branches.path: Sign in with Celonis credentials and complete email-delivered two-factor authentication.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a Celonis identity and email-token step before the selected task.

branches.condition: The team uses SAML or OIDC
branches.path: Authenticate through the company's configured identity provider.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Moves authentication to an external identity provider and organization-specific configuration.

branches.condition: The team administrator enabled open signup
branches.path: Join through the team's persistent signup link and inherit its configured default group permissions.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Makes access possible without a direct invitation but depends on team privacy, seat capacity, and group assignment.

friction_gates.description: The general documentation requires a role and task-family choice before a product route can be followed.
Every user must belong to at least one Celonis Platform team.
Team administrators control user access and permissions, and task capabilities vary by role.
Default Celonis ID sign-in requires an access token sent to the user's email address.
A team's sign-in method can be Celonis ID, SAML, or OIDC.
Product features depend on the team's license, and open signup can consume licensed seats.
The current release notes say the prior data-integration Quickstarts were removed, so those old platform options cannot define a current general route.

sources.title: Getting started with the Celonis Platform
sections_used: Getting started with the Celonis Platform
Setting up and managing your Celonis Platform team
Integrating data sources and capturing user interactions

sources.title: Open signup
evidence_supported: Public or Domain Restricted team privacy requirement

sources.title: Inviting users to your Celonis Platform team
2026 Release Notes
evidence_supported: Reason tied to Business Miner deprecation

uncertainties.question: Which role and task outcome should define Celonis first success?
uncertainties.impact: It changes the actor, prerequisites, product surface, steps, and terminal.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The general official guide explicitly delegates the starting route to business role and lists five different task families.

uncertainties.question: What team, license, permissions, and authentication method would the developer have?
uncertainties.impact: These determine whether the selected feature exists and how the user can enter it.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Celonis documents team-specific access, license-dependent features, and multiple authentication routes, but a general no-account visitor has none of this context.

uncertainties.question: What current guided route replaces the removed data-integration Quickstarts?
uncertainties.impact: A former shortcut cannot be treated as a current first-mile path.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The release notes say the platform options were removed, while the documentation still contains source-specific Objects and Events quickstart pages that assume administrator access and source context.

excluded_after_success: No after-success scope is excluded because the official platform-wide first-success outcome itself has not been selected.
